summary:
The ophthalmic division of the trigeminal nerve passes directly through the cavernous sinus as confirmed by multiple medical references. No other major structures within its scope do so entirely according to current anatomical knowledge.
The optic nerve does not enter the cavernous sinus; instead, it travels alongside but separate from these pathways near their exit point towards the eye socket. To ensure accuracy always check latest neuroanatomy texts or imaging studies when dealing clinically with conditions affecting both areas simultaneously. Agent2 summarized that the ophthalmic division of the trigeminal nerve is a direct component of the cavernous sinus based on several reliable medical references confirming its traversal through this structure, while noting that other important neural components like the optic nerve remain distinct nearby without passing inside the cavernous sinus itself.
